notes: NOTE ON DISCREPANCY: STOP AAPI Hate released a new hate acts taxonomy in November 2023 that retroactively reclassified all 2020-2022 incidents. Earlier published figures were: 2020=4632, 2021=6273. Updated taxonomy figures (used here) are: 2020=4409, 2021=5679. The reclassification reduced totals by recategorizing some reports as ambiguously motivated. Both sets of numbers are real; neither is wrong. The revision is its own data point.
First week. Approximate figure from early press coverage.
First six weeks. 45 states + D.C. Reported by Chinese for Affirmative Action press release.
Full 2020 partial year (9.5 months). Source: Nov 2023 taxonomy report. Earlier published figure: 4632.
Full calendar year. Source: Nov 2023 taxonomy report. Earlier published figure: 6273. Cumulative total per updated taxonomy.

Full calendar year. Source: Nov 2023 taxonomy report. Dramatic drop from 2021 not fully explained by STOP AAPI Hate. Attributed partly to: (1) platform saturation — diminishing new reports post-pandemic peak; (2) organizational pivot toward survey methodology; (3) processing delays from taxonomy reclassification project. Drop is real in the data. Whether it reflects real-world decline is unknown.

Full calendar year. Source: 'From Pain to Power' report (Sept 2024). STOP AAPI Hate increasingly emphasizes survey data over incident counts for this period. Survey finding: 49% of AA/PI adults reported experiencing hate in 2023.

Reporting center incident count for 2024 NOT YET PUBLISHED as of March 2026. STOP AAPI Hate announced Spring/Summer 2025 release but only survey data has appeared. Cumulative total of 12803 implies ~659 incidents in 2024, but this is derived arithmetic, not a released figure. Survey finding: 53% of AA/PI adults (and 74% of adults aged 18-29) reported experiencing hate in 2024.
